front: Director: his Chernobyl-inspired staging of Le Grand Macabre has the chorus in contamination suits lit by a huge lightbulb, which infuriated Gyorgy Ligeti
Director: set a 1980 production of Handel's Orlando in outer space
Director: the soprano Elizabeth Schwarzkopf compared his choices to 'spray[ing] graffiti on the Mona Lisa'
Director: his staging of Messiaen's Saint François d'Assise inspired Kaija Saariaho to write L'Amour de loin, which he also directed
Director: collaborated with Toni Morrison and Rokia Traore on a 2011 work about Desdemona from Othello
Director: created a staged version of Bach's St. Matthew Passion in 2010 for the Berlin Philharmonic; the chorus surrounds the main figure who lies on a wooden box
Director: the Chicano artist Gronk has created hand-painted backdrops for his productions including The Indian Queen and Ainadamar

Opera (libretto): Pasqualita, the Tewa housemaid, sings a song in which 'the cloud-flower blossoms' in each cardinal direction; the main character later quotes 'Batter my heart, three person'd God' at the Trinity test site
Opera (libretto): draws on John Donne's Holy Sonnets, the Bhagavad Gita, government documents including the Smyth Report, and Tewa traditional songs
Opera (libretto): opens with the chorus singing 'We believed that matter can be neither created nor destroyed'
Opera (libretto): making-of documentary is called Wonders are Many
Opera: original staging placed the presidential airplane Spirit of '76 and a giant bomb on stage
back: Nixon in China (Adams, directed by Peter Sellars)

front: Opera (libretto): based on letters from the California Gold Rush; title references Puccini's La fanciulla del West
back: Girls of the Golden West (Adams / Peter Sellars libretto)
work: Girls of the Golden West (Adams) – Libretto

front: Opera: his production was so controversial that the opening scene was later removed from the score
back: The Death of Klinghoffer (Adams, directed by Peter Sellars)

front: Opera: staged as a blaxploitation story in the South Bronx; a boombox replaces the wind ensemble
back: Don Giovanni (Mozart, directed by Peter Sellars)

front: Opera: 1988 production set in a penthouse on the 52nd floor of Trump Tower; influenced by Wye Jamison Allanbrook's analysis of dance rhythms and social class in the work
back: The Marriage of Figaro (Mozart, directed by Peter Sellars)

front: Opera: in 1995, his Covent Garden staging anachronistically included a machine gun and TVs in this Hindemith opera about a Renaissance painter
back: Mathis der Maler (Hindemith, directed by Peter Sellars)
